Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Written Discovery
Off Calendar Sep-16-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Phil Foster appeared by zoom for Jeanne Kwong (Respondent).
Douglas Robbins appeared by zoom for Jennifer Kwok (Petitioner).
Daniel Bernhard appeared by zoom for the non-parties.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to the issue of whether or not the petitioner is going for double recovery, whether the petitioner is going for damages that have already been paid, whether the damages are the same or different, whether the causes of action are the same or different, whether or not the facts of both cases are different, the issue of maintaining confidentiality with respect to the settlement agreement, etc. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirms its tentative ruling. Respondent's Motion to Compel Further Response to Written Discovery is denied. There is insufficient showing that Respondent and Non-parties Freeland Cooper & Foreman, LLP, Daniel T. Bernhard and Kate C. Freeland are joint tortfeasors for purposes of Code of Civil Procedure section 877. The claims in Petitioner's malpractice action (Kwok, et al. v. Freeland, Cooper & Foreman LLP, et. al. SCSF Case No. CGC-19-576188) and Petitioner's Verified Second Amended Petition for Breach of Trust, Constructive Fraud, and Removal of Trustee are not for the same wrong or injury as required to receive credit under Code of Civil Procedure section 877. See In re JTS Corp (2016) 617 F.3d 1102 and Lafayette v. County of Los Angeles (1984) 162 Cal. App.3d 547. Petitioner's sanctions request is denied.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-17-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Phil Foster, Esq. appearing by CourtCall for Jeanne Kwong (Moving Party).
Denise Mejlszenkier, Esq. appearing by Zoom/CourtCall for Jennifer Kwok.

Matter called, argument on JEANNE KWONG'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows: Respondent's Motion for Further Response to Request for Document Production and for Sanctions is denied. There is insufficient showing that the amount of attorney's fees Petitioner has accrued over the previous twelve years is relevant to determining whether the amount of attorney's fees Respondent is seeking to have borne by the Trust is reasonable and necessary (Donahue v. Donahue (2010) 182 Cal. App. 4th 259). Further, per case law, the contents of the requested invoices is entirely covered by the attorney-client communications privilege (Los Angeles Cty. Bd. of Supervisors v. Sup. Ct (2016) 2 Cal. 5th 282).

The competing sanctions requests are both denied. Respondent did not prevail on this motion, and is therefore not entitled to sanctions. Because Petitioner failed to comply with CCP section 2031.240, the establishment of the privilege claim was left to this motion. Accordingly, Petitioner's request for sanctions is also denied.

Ms. Mejlszenkier is to prepare the form order pursuant to CRC 3.312 and then submit it to the Court for review and signature.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 292733 The Estate of Stanley Chong Kwong.

Appearances: Carl Lippenberger for Lau Kwong and Larry Kwong (Creditors). Daniel Bernhard and Eli Coffino-Greenberg for Jennifer Shik-Han Kwok. Paul Tour-Sarkissian for Jeanne Kwong.

The Court suggested that Counsel discuss the matter outside of the Courtroom. Upon returning, Counsel reported that they did not reach an agreement. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the Creditors' Claims being untimely, the validity of the Disclaimer, the Creditors' Claim being mooted by the Disclaimer, the Promissory Notes being in the Corporation Trust and the intent of the Decedent. The Court expressed concern about the notes not being paid. The Court took the Creditors' Claim under submission (orders to be submitted by Mr. Tour-Sarkissian). Mr. Bernhard requested an evidentiary hearing on the 850 petition. The Court suggested Counsel discuss whether the by-pass Trust can be funded by the other assets, talk about what those other assets might be and narrow down the issues. The 850 Petition was continued to June 8, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a status conference.
